children.text: Long Lots is one of Westport’s established residential areas, where the lifestyle tends to be defined by neighborhood routine rather than nightlife or retail foot traffic. It’s calm, consistent, and feels like “classic Westport” in the best sense.

Housing is predominantly single-family, and the feel is steady and residential. In my experience, Long Lots often works well for buyers who want Westport’s broader amenities and community, but who prefer to live in a quieter, more traditional neighborhood setting.

From Long Lots, you’re still well-positioned for Westport’s signature lifestyle: downtown dining and shopping, commuter rail access via Westport station on the New Haven Line, and shoreline time at Compo Beach. Long Lots is best for buyers who want an established residential cadence with convenient access to the rest of Westport.

highlights: A classic, established Westport residential neighborhood
Predominantly single-family housing with a steady, neighborhood-oriented feel
Convenient access to downtown Westport amenities and Main Street
Westport Metro-North (New Haven Line) within reach for commuting
Easy access to beaches and parks, including Compo Beach
name: Long Lots
overview: Long Lots is a classic residential section of Westport—quiet, established, and oriented around a comfortable day-to-day family routine. Homes are primarily single-family, and the neighborhood feel is steady and community-forward rather than destination-driven. You’re close enough to downtown Westport for dining and shopping, and Westport’s shoreline and parks are still part of the lifestyle. For buyers who want Westport with a traditional neighborhood cadence, Long Lots fits well.
